vocabulary.translation: bullicioso / lleno de actividad
vocabulary.example: The bustling city centre was full of tourists and locals.

vocabulary.translation: vibrante / lleno de vida y energía
vocabulary.example: The neighbourhood has a vibrant arts scene.

vocabulary.translation: animado / con mucha energía
vocabulary.example: The market is lively every Saturday morning.

vocabulary.translation: cosmopolita / diverso y abierto al mundo
vocabulary.example: London is one of the most cosmopolitan cities in the world.

vocabulary.translation: frenético / con ritmo de vida estresante
vocabulary.example: Life in the capital can be hectic and exhausting.

vocabulary.translation: pintoresco / visualmente encantador
vocabulary.example: We walked through a picturesque old quarter.

vocabulary.translation: vasto / extenso y desordenado sobre un área grande
vocabulary.example: The sprawling suburbs stretch for miles in every direction.

vocabulary.translation: próspero / rico
vocabulary.example: They moved to an affluent neighbourhood on the outskirts.

vocabulary.translation: prometedor / en auge, que mejora rápidamente
vocabulary.example: It's an up-and-coming area that's becoming very popular.

vocabulary.translation: congestionado / lleno de tráfico o gente
vocabulary.example: The roads are heavily congested during rush hour.

vocabulary.translation: impecable / extremadamente limpio
vocabulary.example: The streets were spotless — not a trace of rubbish anywhere.

vocabulary.translation: bien gestionado / organizado eficientemente
vocabulary.example: It's a well-run city with excellent public services.

vocabulary.translation: desfavorecido / que carece de recursos básicos
vocabulary.example: Many families in deprived areas lack access to good schools.

vocabulary.translation: descuidado / deteriorado por falta de mantenimiento
vocabulary.example: The city centre looked run-down with peeling paint everywhere.

vocabulary.translation: caótico / desordenado y sin control
vocabulary.example: The traffic was chaotic during the festival.

vocabulary.translation: contaminado / con aire, agua o tierra sucios
vocabulary.example: The river running through the city is heavily polluted.

vocabulary.translation: compacto / pequeño y bien organizado
vocabulary.example: It's a compact city — you can walk almost everywhere.

vocabulary.translation: muy turístico / lleno de turistas
vocabulary.example: The old town feels a bit touristy in summer.

vocabulary.translation: abarrotado / lleno de gente
vocabulary.example: The underground is always crowded at rush hour.

vocabulary.translation: antiguo / de muy lejana antigüedad
vocabulary.example: The city has an ancient historical centre that dates back 300 years.

vocabulary.translation: histórico / de gran importancia en la historia
vocabulary.example: We stayed in the historic quarter near the cathedral.

vocabulary.translation: contemporáneo / actual, de nuestro tiempo
vocabulary.example: The museum focuses on contemporary urban art.

vocabulary.translation: brotar / aparecer o construirse rápidamente
vocabulary.example: New shops are springing up all over the place.

vocabulary.translation: mucho entusiasmo / energía en el ambiente
vocabulary.example: There's a real buzz about the place since the renovation.

vocabulary.translation: detenerse por completo (especialmente el tráfico)
vocabulary.example: Traffic ground to a halt after the accident on the motorway.

vocabulary.translation: asfixiarse con los gases del escape
vocabulary.example: Cyclists were choking on the fumes in the tunnel.

vocabulary.translation: ni rastro de basura
vocabulary.example: The streets were immaculate — not a trace of rubbish.

vocabulary.translation: establecerse / mudarse para vivir a largo plazo
vocabulary.example: Many young professionals are settling down in this neighbourhood.

vocabulary.translation: en las afueras / en la periferia
vocabulary.example: They built a new shopping centre on the outskirts of the city.

vocabulary.translation: tocar el claxon / la bocina
vocabulary.example: Frustrated drivers were sounding their horns in the traffic jam.

vocabulary.translation: oscuro y de aspecto sucio / de mala muerte
vocabulary.example: The underpass was dark and dingy and felt unsafe at night.

vocabulary.translation: abarrotado / muy lleno o apretado
vocabulary.example: The streets were crammed with people during the festival.

vocabulary.translation: clase media
vocabulary.example: The middle class tends to move in as an area gentrifies.

vocabulary.translation: centro de la ciudad
vocabulary.example: The city centre has been pedestrianised to reduce traffic.

vocabulary.translation: atracador / ladrón que asalta en la calle
vocabulary.example: She was warned about muggers in that part of the city.

vocabulary.translation: invertir fuertemente / grandes sumas de dinero
vocabulary.example: The council invested heavily in the new transport infrastructure.

vocabulary.translation: infraestructura de transporte (carreteras, vías, estaciones)
vocabulary.example: Improving transport infrastructure is key to reducing congestion.

vocabulary.translation: funcionar a la perfección / sin interrupciones
vocabulary.example: Since the upgrade, the metro system runs very smoothly.

vocabulary.translation: experimentar gentrificación / transformación de barrio humilde a clase media-alta
vocabulary.example: The area is experiencing gentrification as new cafés and galleries open.

vocabulary.translation: urbanizaciones / complejos residenciales
vocabulary.example: New residential developments are springing up on the outskirts.

vocabulary.translation: algo descuidado / parcialmente abandonado
vocabulary.example: The park had been somewhat neglected for years.

vocabulary.translation: edificios en ruinas / abandonados
vocabulary.example: The council plans to demolish the derelict buildings near the station.

vocabulary.translation: pintura desconchada / que se está cayendo
vocabulary.example: The walls were covered in peeling paint and graffiti.

vocabulary.translation: apartamentos en urbanización privada con seguridad
vocabulary.example: The new gated apartments contrast sharply with the surrounding area.

vocabulary.translation: al lado de / junto a
vocabulary.example: Luxury flats were built alongside affordable social housing.

vocabulary.translation: niebla contaminante / smog
vocabulary.example: The city was covered in smog on windless winter days.

vocabulary.translation: tirar basura al suelo
vocabulary.example: Littering is subject to heavy fines in Singapore.

vocabulary.translation: ayuntamiento / consejo municipal
vocabulary.example: The council approved the new housing development last month.

vocabulary.translation: consulta / proceso de recogida de opiniones
vocabulary.example: The project went through a public consultation before approval.

vocabulary.translation: prever / tener en mente como plan o posibilidad
vocabulary.example: The architects envisage a fully pedestrian city centre by 2030.

vocabulary.translation: materializarse / llegar a hacerse realidad
vocabulary.example: Unfortunately, the promised investment never materialized.

vocabulary.translation: demoler / derribar un edificio
vocabulary.example: They plan to demolish the old factory and build a park.

vocabulary.translation: desde cero / sin nada previo
vocabulary.example: The new district was built entirely from scratch.

vocabulary.translation: proyecto piloto / prueba a pequeña escala
vocabulary.example: They ran a pilot scheme in one neighbourhood before expanding it.

vocabulary.translation: ampliar / llevar algo a mayor escala
vocabulary.example: Once the pilot worked, they scaled it up to the whole city.

vocabulary.translation: próspero / que crece y funciona muy bien
vocabulary.example: The city now has a thriving tech industry.

vocabulary.translation: aprovechar / canalizar una fuente de energía o recurso
vocabulary.example: The city plans to harness solar energy to power public transport.

vocabulary.translation: renovar / restaurar un edificio o zona
vocabulary.example: The council renovated the old market building into a cultural centre.

vocabulary.translation: vida sostenible / estilo de vida respetuoso con el medio ambiente
vocabulary.example: The new development was designed with sustainable living in mind.

vocabulary.translation: tecnología verde / tecnología respetuosa con el medio ambiente
vocabulary.example: The city invested in green technology to reduce emissions.

vocabulary.translation: energías renovables
vocabulary.example: The plan is to power the city entirely on renewables by 2040.

vocabulary.translation: servicios e instalaciones / comodidades urbanas
vocabulary.example: The neighbourhood lacks basic amenities like parks and shops.

vocabulary.translation: referente / faro, ejemplo a seguir
vocabulary.example: The city became a beacon of sustainable urban planning.

vocabulary.translation: inversión externa / capital que entra en una zona desde fuera
vocabulary.example: The tax breaks attracted significant inward investment.

vocabulary.translation: economía post-petróleo / economía sin dependencia del petróleo
vocabulary.example: The city is positioning itself as a leader in the post-oil economy.

vocabulary.translation: de arriba abajo / impuesto desde el gobierno o autoridad
vocabulary.example: Critics argued the plan was too top-down and ignored residents.

vocabulary.translation: a pequeña escala
vocabulary.example: They tested the scheme on a small scale before rolling it out.

vocabulary.translation: emprendió / se comprometió a hacer algo (pasado de undertake)
vocabulary.example: The city undertook a major renovation of its historic centre.

vocabulary.translation: reconocido / al que se atribuye el mérito de algo
vocabulary.example: The mayor is credited with transforming the waterfront area.

vocabulary.translation: inyectar / invertir grandes cantidades de dinero
vocabulary.example: The government pumped millions into the regeneration project.

vocabulary.translation: leyenda urbana
vocabulary.example: The story about alligators in the sewers is a classic urban legend.

vocabulary.word: passed on by word of mouth
vocabulary.translation: transmitido de boca en boca
vocabulary.example: Urban myths are typically passed on by word of mouth.

vocabulary.translation: de generación en generación
vocabulary.example: These stories have been told from generation to generation.

vocabulary.translation: tener una moraleja / transmitir un principio
vocabulary.example: Most fairy tales carry a moral about right and wrong.

vocabulary.translation: trama / argumento de una historia
vocabulary.example: The plot of the urban legend always has a shocking twist.

vocabulary.translation: dar un aire de / aparentar
vocabulary.example: The detail in the story gives it an air of authenticity.

vocabulary.translation: herir gravemente / mutilar
vocabulary.example: In the legend, the creature maimed anyone who got too close.

vocabulary.translation: rumor
vocabulary.example: The rumour spread quickly through social media.

vocabulary.translation: conspiración
vocabulary.example: Some people believe the story is part of a wider conspiracy.

vocabulary.translation: alcantarillado / cloaca
vocabulary.example: The legend claims that giant rats live in the city sewers.

vocabulary.translation: misógino / con actitud de odio hacia las mujeres
vocabulary.example: Some critics argue the story has misogynistic undertones.

vocabulary.translation: generalizado / muy extendido
vocabulary.example: The belief in this myth is surprisingly widespread.

vocabulary.translation: neutralizar parcialmente / contrarrestar en parte
vocabulary.example: The campaign partly counteracted the spread of misinformation.

vocabulary.translation: mirándolo más de cerca / si se analiza con detalle
vocabulary.example: On closer examination, the story falls apart completely.

vocabulary.translation: noticias falsas / desinformación
vocabulary.example: Many urban legends are the fake news of previous centuries.

vocabulary.translation: en general / en líneas generales
vocabulary.example: By and large, the plan was well received by residents.

vocabulary.translation: controles y contrapesos / sistema de equilibrio de poderes
vocabulary.example: A democracy needs proper checks and balances to function.

vocabulary.translation: claro y resuelto / sin ambigüedad
vocabulary.example: The situation isn't as cut and dried as it seems.

vocabulary.translation: por todas partes / de lejos y de cerca
vocabulary.example: People came from far and wide to see the new development.

vocabulary.translation: ante todo / en primer lugar y principalmente
vocabulary.example: First and foremost, we need to address the housing crisis.

vocabulary.translation: aquí y allá / en varios sitios
vocabulary.example: There were signs of neglect here and there throughout the estate.

vocabulary.translation: irregular / a veces bien y a veces mal
vocabulary.example: The quality of the renovation work was rather hit and miss.

vocabulary.translation: ajetreo / bullicio y actividad intensa
vocabulary.example: She loves the hustle and bustle of city life.

vocabulary.translation: ley y orden / seguridad y cumplimiento de las normas
vocabulary.example: The new mayor promised to restore law and order in the streets.

vocabulary.translation: por ahí / activo y moviéndose por la ciudad
vocabulary.example: On sunny days, everyone is out and about in the park.

vocabulary.translation: parte integral / algo inseparable de otra cosa
vocabulary.example: Traffic congestion is part and parcel of city living.

vocabulary.translation: auge y caída / ascenso y declive
vocabulary.example: The documentary explores the rise and fall of industrial cities.

vocabulary.translation: normas y reglamentos / conjunto de reglas oficiales
vocabulary.example: Developers must comply with all the rules and regulations.

vocabulary.translation: sano y salvo / sin daño alguno
vocabulary.example: The children arrived home safe and sound.

vocabulary.translation: el buen camino / conducta correcta y honesta
vocabulary.example: The youth programme aims to keep teenagers on the straight and narrow.

vocabulary.translation: de un lado a otro / de ida y vuelta
vocabulary.example: Commuters travel to and fro between the suburbs and the city.

vocabulary.translation: violencia y terror / contenido perturbador
vocabulary.example: Urban legends often involve scenes of violence and horror.

vocabulary.translation: se prevé que se complete / está proyectado para terminarse
vocabulary.example: The new metro line is projected to be completed by 2027.

vocabulary.translation: está previsto que sea / tiene como objetivo ser
vocabulary.example: The park is intended to be a green space for all residents.

vocabulary.translation: está previsto que aumente / se espera que suba
vocabulary.example: The population is set to rise significantly over the next decade.

vocabulary.translation: se espera que haya alcanzado
vocabulary.example: By 2030, the city is expected to have reached carbon neutrality.

vocabulary.translation: se esperaba que proporcionaran
vocabulary.example: The new buildings were expected to provide 500 affordable homes.

vocabulary.translation: un amplio abanico de / una gran variedad de
vocabulary.example: The city offers a wide range of cultural activities.

vocabulary.translation: de manera similar / del mismo modo
vocabulary.example: Similarly, other cities have faced the same urban challenges.

vocabulary.translation: un término popularizado por
vocabulary.example: It's a term popularized by academics in the 1980s.

vocabulary.translation: abarca un amplio abanico de
vocabulary.example: The term encompasses a wide range of social phenomena.

vocabulary.translation: mirándolo más de cerca / analizándolo con detalle
vocabulary.example: On closer examination, the data tells a different story.

vocabulary.translation: hasta cierto punto / en cierta medida
vocabulary.example: To some extent, the problem is caused by poor planning.

vocabulary.translation: para contextualizar / para dar más información de fondo
vocabulary.example: For context, the city's population doubled in just 20 years.

vocabulary.translation: más o menos / aproximadamente
vocabulary.example: The project cost ten million euros, give or take.

vocabulary.translation: por así decirlo / en cierto sentido
vocabulary.example: The city is the engine room, so to speak, of the national economy.

vocabulary.translation: también conocido como
vocabulary.example: The phenomenon, also known as urban sprawl, affects most big cities.

vocabulary.translation: de cerca / en detalle
vocabulary.example: Let's look at this in close to understand the real impact.

vocabulary.translation: que se remonta a / que tiene su origen en
vocabulary.example: The city has a historical centre that dates back 300 years.
